hook: AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-line answer. Again.
caption:
AI gave you a 5-paragraph essay when you needed a one-line answer. Again.

Here's what's actually happening:

ChatGPT doesn't know if you're a "give me the full context" person or a "just tell me the answer" person. So it guesses. And it usually guesses wrong for you specifically.

Some people read every word of a long AI response and feel respected.

You read the first sentence, saw the wall of text, and closed the tab.

Neither reaction is wrong. You just have completely different information diets.

The myth is that AI is "smart enough" to figure this out on its own if you just prompt it better.

The reality: prompting harder doesn't fix a model that has no memory of who you are. You're explaining yourself from scratch every single conversation.

That's not a prompting problem. That's a context problem.

Your BootFile carries that context so you stop re-training AI on your personality every time you open a new chat.
